title: Lab 39: SSH Key Authentication and Rsync
description: Configure key-based SSH access and securely transfer files between systems

tasks: On client, ensure the user mesh39 exists on both client and server, and set the password on both systems to cinder9.
On client, as user mesh39, generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase.
On client, configure passwordless SSH access for mesh39 from client to server using public key authentication.
On client, using rsync over SSH, copy the directory /home/mesh39/client-data/ from client to /home/mesh39/server-data/ on server.
hints: Use the mesh39 account for the SSH key work.
Verify the transfer by checking the copied file on server through SSH.
checks: # server getent passwd mesh39 >/dev/null && getent shadow mesh39 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/ {ok=1} END {exit !ok}'
runuser -l mesh39 -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no mesh39@192.168.122.3 true'
solution_outline: Use ssh-keygen, authorized_keys, and rsync over SSH.
task_titles: Ensure mesh39 exists on both systems
generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase
Configure passwordless SSH access for mesh39 from...
Using rsync over SSH, copy the directory...
